source.page_label: 2026-2027 North Dakota Hunting Seasons | ND Game and Fish
source.update_note: 2026-27 migratory game bird data from North Dakota Game and Fish season dates page. Many 2026-27 waterfowl dates are listed as Tentative by NDGF pending proclamation signing. Nonresident waterfowl hunters must select two 7-day periods in different zones. HIP registration required for all migratory bird hunters.

species.name: Duck (including Mergansers)
species.season_raw: Resident: Sept 26 – Dec 8; Nonresident: Oct 5 – Dec 8 (tentative)
species.hunting_units: High Plains Unit and Low Plains Unit (see sub-seasons)

sub_seasons.name: Resident — High Plains & Low Plains Units
Nonresident — High Plains & Low Plains Units

species.name: Coot (American Coot)
species.season_raw: Same as duck season — Resident: Sept 26; Nonresident: Oct 5 (tentative)
species.hunting_units: High Plains Unit and Low Plains Unit

species.name: Canada Goose
species.season_raw: Early: Aug 15 – Sep 15; Regular Resident: Sep 26 – Dec 16; Regular Nonresident: Oct 5 – Dec 16 (tentative)
species.hunting_units: Missouri River, Western, and Eastern Zones (see sub-seasons)
species.bag_limit: Varies by zone and season (see sub-seasons)

sub_seasons.name: Early Canada Goose (August Management / Early September)

species.name: Light Geese (Snow, Blue, Ross's)
species.season_raw: Regular Resident: Sep 26 – Jan 4; Regular Nonresident: Oct 5 – Jan 4; Spring Conservation Order: Feb 21 – May 10, 2027

species.name: White-fronted Goose
species.season_raw: Resident: Sep 26 – Dec 16; Nonresident: Oct 5 – Dec 16 (tentative)

species.name: Youth Waterfowl
species.bag_limit: Same as regular season limits for ducks, geese, coots

key_notes.title: HIP (Harvest Information Program) registration is required annually and free for all hunters (any age) hunting ducks, geese, swans, mergansers, coots, cranes, snipe, doves, and woodcock.

sources.label: NDGF — HIP Registration
sources.evidence: HIP Registration (free): Required annually for all hunters (any age) hunting ducks, geese, swans, mergansers, coots, cranes, snipe, doves, and woodcock.

key_notes.title: A Federal Duck Stamp ($25 + $4 mailing) is required for all waterfowl hunters age 16 or older (ducks, geese, swans, mergansers, brant, coot). Not required during Spring Light Goose Conservation Order.

sources.label: NDGF — Nonresident Licenses
sources.evidence: Federal Waterfowl Stamp ($25 + $4 mailing): Required for hunters 16+ for ducks, geese, swans, mergansers, brant, coot. Exception: Not needed during spring light goose conservation season.

key_notes.title: Nonresident waterfowl hunters are restricted to two 7-day hunting periods per year, each in a different nonresident waterfowl zone (NE, NC, NW, SE, SC, SW). If hunting only one 7-day period, may select two zones during that period.

sources.label: NDGF — Nonresident Licenses and Requirements
sources.evidence: Nonresidents may hunt waterfowl for two 7-day periods per year. Six nonresident waterfowl hunting zones. Each 7-day period must be in a different zone. The same zone cannot be used for both periods.

key_notes.title: Duck bag limits have species-specific restrictions: 6 daily in aggregate. Typical species limits include mallards, pintails, canvasbacks, redheads, and scaup restrictions. Consult NDGF regulations for full details.

sources.label: NDGF — Small Game, Waterfowl and Furbearer Proclamation
sources.evidence: The daily duck bag limit, including species restrictions and all other regulations, shall be 6 per day.

key_notes.title: Sandhill Crane Unit 1 and Unit 2 have different bag limits. Unit 1: 3 daily / 9 possession. Unit 2: 2 daily / 6 possession.

sources.label: NDGF — Hunting Bag Limits
sources.evidence: Sandhill Crane: 3 per day, 9 in possession (Unit 1), 2 per day, 6 in possession (Unit 2).

key_notes.title: Shooting hours for all migratory game birds: one-half hour before sunrise to sunset. Non-toxic shot required for all waterfowl and migratory bird hunting.

sources.label: NDGF — Hunting Seasons
sources.evidence: Standard shooting hours and non-toxic shot requirements apply. Check NDGF regulations for full details.
